meta.title: HSE Skills Roadmap — Re-roofing Block C
meta.subtitle: Recommended skill chain + de-identified run sheet (catalog router)
meta.author: using-hse-skills (catalog router)
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label ([SITE-1], [ROLE: site manager], [CONTRACTOR]). The captured handoff facts were pseudonymized before this roadmap was written; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: How this works — read me first

Routing for: re-roofing fall-from-height at [SITE-1], own workers + [CONTRACTOR], UK, construction. Goal: make the job safe end to end — risk assessment, safe-work method, permit, and a toolbox talk the crew can sign. This router produces no HSE artifact itself; it recommends the ordered chain below and hands the de-identified context over. Run Step 1 in the chat where this roadmap was produced; for each later step, open a fresh chat, paste the Context Capsule, and attach the prior step's already-de-identified output. A fresh chat per step keeps each one fast and focused.
This rendered roadmap is your durable Steps-2+ plan — save it and keep it to hand. Run Step 1 only when you are ready; the later steps are here so the plan survives Step 1 filling the chat.
SEQUENCE MAP:  risk-assessment → job-safety-analysis → permit-to-work → toolbox-talk

rows: Establishes the hazard/control baseline for fall-from-height before any method or permit.
Breaks the agreed control set into a step-by-step safe-work method for the crew.
Authorises the work-at-height task with the Step 1–2 controls as permit conditions.
Briefs and signs off the night crew on the specific hazards and controls before they start.
sections.caption: Ordered recommendation with per-skill rationale (ROUTE-03)

sections.text: Context Capsule — paste at the top of each fresh chat (Steps 2+)

Job: strip-and-re-roof Block C at [SITE-1], fall-from-height from the eaves. Jurisdiction: UK · Industry: construction · Exposed: own workers + [CONTRACTOR]. Lead: [ROLE: site manager]. Goal: risk assessment → safe-work method → permit → toolbox talk. SEQUENCE MAP: risk-assessment → job-safety-analysis → permit-to-work → toolbox-talk. Role labels only — the re-identification key is held separately, not in this capsule.
Step 1 — risk-assessment (runs in place — no copy-paste)

items: WHY: Establishes the hazard/control baseline for the re-roofing task at height.
RUN: /risk-assessment (or: use the risk-assessment skill) — in the chat where this roadmap was produced. Step 1 is not a fresh-chat block.
DELTA: full task steps (access → strip → load-out → re-lay → edge-protect) and the 5×5 matrix.
DEPENDENCY: Independent — the first step.
FEEDS →: Step 2 (the agreed control set + residual risks).
WHY: Turns the agreed controls into a step-by-step safe-work method for the crew.
RUN: /job-safety-analysis.
REFERENCE: Paste the Context Capsule above first — the skill's intake confirms it and asks only the JSA-specific details, so you do not re-enter the site or crew.
DELTA: the agreed control set; the task broken into method steps.
DEPENDENCY: Dependent — run after Step 1.
FEEDS →: Step 3 (the method steps + control owners).

sections.text: ATTACH — Step 2: the risk-assessment OUTPUT from Step 1 — the control set + residual risks (already de-identified by the risk-assessment skill). The JSA is built from these.

items: WHY: Authorises the work-at-height task with the Step 1–2 controls as conditions.
RUN: /permit-to-work.
REFERENCE: Paste the Context Capsule above first — no need to re-enter the shared context.
DELTA: the working-at-height permit type and the validity window.
DEPENDENCY: Dependent — run after Steps 1 and 2.
FEEDS →: Step 4 (the permitted controls + sign-on conditions).

sections.text: ATTACH — Step 3: the job-safety-analysis OUTPUT from Step 2 — the method steps + control owners — and the risk-assessment residual risks from Step 1 (both already de-identified).

items: WHY: Briefs and signs off the night crew on the specific hazards and controls.
RUN: /toolbox-talk.
REFERENCE: Paste the Context Capsule above first — the crew, site, and chain are carried by it.
DELTA: tonight's crew and the top three controls to brief.
DEPENDENCY: Dependent — run after Step 3.
FEEDS →: none — final step; the crew signs and starts work.

sections.text: ATTACH — Step 4: the permit-to-work OUTPUT from Step 3 — the permitted controls + sign-on conditions the crew must acknowledge (already de-identified by the permit-to-work skill).

items: Step 1, here: on a host with the Skill tool, say "go" and the router launches the risk-assessment skill in this chat seeded with the confirmed facts; off-platform, paste the printed Step-1 block into this same chat — Step 1 is never a fresh-chat block.
Steps 2–4, fresh chat each: open a new chat, paste the Context Capsule at the top, run the named skill, and attach the prior step's already-de-identified output as the ATTACH note instructs.
Can't carry the capsule between chats? Ask to expand any step to standalone and the router inlines the full shared context into that one step so it self-runs without the capsule.
Each target skill's own intake echoes the capsule facts back and asks only the deep, per-artifact details this router deferred (scoring, baseline, evidence, obligations, competency, cadence).
